name: Rejoice, ye Gentiles
notes: In Romans 15:9-12 Paul gathers four Old Testament witnesses to show that the Gentiles were always meant to praise God together with His people. He draws first from the Psalter (Psalm 18:49, the same song recorded in 2 Samuel 22:50), then from the Law (Deuteronomy 32:43), then again from the Psalter (Psalm 117:1), and finally from the Prophets (Isaiah 11:10), so that Law, Psalms, and Prophets all speak with one voice. The shared core is the summons for the nations to confess, rejoice in, and praise the LORD alongside Israel, rooted in the Gentiles seeking the root of Jesse. Each citation keeps Paul's own purpose-statement in Romans 15:9, "that the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy," while the source texts carry their fuller original setting. Psalm 18:49 and 2 Samuel 22:50 are David's one song preserved twice, so both are linked to Paul's single quotation in Romans 15:9.

links.label: praise the LORD all ye ... all ye people
there shall be a root of Jesse ... the Gentiles
